source: BERDE, LEED v4.1, RA 9003 (Ecological Solid Waste Management), PGBC

rules.description: Minimum percentage of materials sourced within 160km of site
rules.notes: Reduces transport emissions and supports local economy. Philippines has strong local production of concrete, hollow blocks, steel, bamboo, and hardwood.

rules.description: Minimum recycled content in building materials by cost
rules.notes: BERDE baseline. Include recycled steel (typically 25-90% recycled), recycled aggregate concrete, reclaimed wood. Post-consumer recycled content valued at 2× pre-consumer.

rules.description: Rapidly renewable materials as percentage of total material cost
rules.notes: Materials with harvest cycle ≤10 years: bamboo (3-5yr), coconut lumber, rattan, cork, straw, natural fiber insulation. Philippines has abundant bamboo and coconut resources.

rules.description: All virgin wood must be legally sourced and preferably FSC/PEFC certified
rules.notes: RA 7586 and DENR regulations prohibit use of illegally sourced timber. FSC certification preferred. Alternative: reclaimed/salvaged wood (superior green credit).

rules.description: All interior adhesives, sealants, paints must be low-VOC
rules.notes: VOC limits per SCAQMD or GreenSeal standards. Paints ≤50 g/L, adhesives ≤70 g/L, sealants ≤250 g/L. Critical in tropical climates where heat accelerates off-gassing.

rules.description: Environmental Product Declarations (EPD) required for major materials
rules.notes: BERDE and LEED award credits for EPDs covering 20+ permanently installed products. Enables lifecycle carbon accounting.

rules.description: Concrete optimization — target cement reduction strategies
rules.notes: Concrete is 8% of global CO2. Reduce through: supplementary cementitious materials (fly ash, GGBS, rice husk ash), optimized mix design, post-tensioning for thinner sections.

rules.description: Whole-building lifecycle assessment (LCA) for BERDE certification
rules.notes: LCA covers cradle-to-grave: material extraction, transport, construction, operation, demolition. Demonstrate 10% reduction vs. baseline for BERDE credit.

rules.description: Minimum construction waste diversion from landfill
rules.notes: By weight. Segregate: concrete/masonry (crush for fill), metals (recycle), wood (reuse/biomass), packaging (recycle). Requires waste management plan before construction.

rules.description: Construction waste management plan required before groundbreaking
rules.notes: Plan identifies: waste streams, diversion targets, responsible parties, haulers, tracking method. BERDE prerequisite.

rules.description: Maximum construction waste generation per floor area
rules.notes: Philippine average: 40-60 kg/m². Target: ≤25 kg/m² through prefabrication, modular construction, cut optimization, and material take-off accuracy.

rules.description: Waste segregation facilities required per RA 9003
rules.notes: RA 9003 mandates segregation into: biodegradable, recyclable, residual, special/hazardous. Building must provide designated collection points for each stream.

rules.description: Composting facility for buildings with food service or >50 residential units
rules.notes: On-site composting or vermicomposting for organic waste. Reduces hauling volume by 40-60%. Compost used for landscaping.

rules.description: Recycling storage area minimum size per building area
rules.notes: Dedicated, accessible, covered, and ventilated recycling storage. Minimum 10sqm for any commercial building.
